name: atlassian
description: Full-featured Atlassian Cloud integration via the official Rovo MCP Server. Manage Jira issues, Confluence pages, Compass components, JSM alerts, and Bitbucket repositories. Supports OAuth 2.0 (3LO) and API token authentication with per-product enablement.
author: OrionOmega

setup.description: Authenticate with Atlassian Cloud to access Jira, Confluence, Compass, JSM, and Bitbucket via the Rovo MCP Server.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (3LO) — recommended
methods.description: Use Atlassian's three-legged OAuth flow. Requires an OAuth app registered at developer.atlassian.com. Supports all Rovo MCP Server tools including Compass.

methods.label: Personal API Token (Basic auth)
methods.description: Use a personal API token for headless/unattended access. Some tools (e.g. Compass) may be unavailable.

tools.name: atlassian_jira
tools.description: Manage Jira issues: get, create, edit, transition, comment, search via JQL, list projects and issue types. Covers read_jira, write_jira, and search_jira permission groups.

project_key.description: Jira project key (e.g. 'PROJ') for create/list operations

description.description: Issue description (ADF or plain text) for create/edit

fields.description: Additional fields to set (for create/edit). Keys are field IDs or names.

query.description: User name or email to look up (for lookup_user)

cloud_id.description: Atlassian Cloud site ID. If omitted, uses the default from settings.

tools.name: atlassian_confluence
tools.description: Manage Confluence pages and spaces: get, create, update pages, list spaces, search via CQL, manage comments. Covers read_confluence, write_confluence, and search_confluence permission groups.

body.description: Page body content in storage format or wiki markup (for create/update)

inline_text_selection.description: Text selection to anchor inline comment to

cloud_id.description: Atlassian Cloud site ID. If omitted, uses the default from settings.

tools.name: atlassian_compass
tools.description: Manage Compass components: get, create, list, search, view activity, labels, types, relationships, and custom fields. Requires OAuth 2.0 authentication.

fields.description: Additional fields for create/custom field operations

cloud_id.description: Atlassian Cloud site ID. If omitted, uses the default from settings.

tools.name: atlassian_jsm
tools.description: Manage Jira Service Management operations: get alerts, list on-call schedules, view teams, update alert status. Requires API token authentication.

cloud_id.description: Atlassian Cloud site ID. If omitted, uses the default from settings.

tools.name: atlassian_bitbucket
tools.description: Manage Bitbucket Cloud repositories, pull requests, pipelines, and deployments. Requires API token authentication with Bitbucket scopes.

destination_branch.description: Destination branch for PR creation (default: main)

cloud_id.description: Atlassian Cloud site ID. If omitted, uses the default from settings.

tools.name: atlassian_search
tools.description: Cross-product Atlassian search and Teamwork Graph queries. Search across Jira and Confluence using natural language (Rovo), or traverse the Teamwork Graph for connected context.

entity_ari.description: Entity ARI to get connected context for (for get_context)

cloud_id.description: Atlassian Cloud site ID. If omitted, uses the default from settings.

_auth_header.label: ── Authentication ──
_auth_header.description: Choose your authentication method and enter your credentials below.

auth_method.label: Authentication Method
auth_method.description: How to authenticate with Atlassian Cloud. OAuth 2.0 (3LO) supports all products; API Token is simpler but may not support Compass.

_oauth_instructions.label: OAuth 2.0 Setup Instructions
_oauth_instructions.description:
To configure OAuth 2.0 (3LO):

1. Go to https://developer.atlassian.com/console/myapps/
2. Create a new app (or select your existing one)
3. Under 'Authorization', click 'Configure' next to 'OAuth 2.0 (3LO)'
4. Set the Callback URL to: http://<your-server-host>:5000/api/gateway/skills/atlassian/oauth/callback
   (The settings panel below shows the exact URL for your current server — copy it from there)
5. Under 'Permissions', add the Jira API, Confluence API, and any others you need
6. Add the required scopes for each API
7. Under 'Settings', copy the Client ID and Secret below
8. Click 'Authorize with Atlassian' below to complete the OAuth flow

oauth_client_id.label: OAuth Client ID
oauth_client_id.description: The Client ID from your Atlassian OAuth app. Find this under Settings in the Atlassian Developer Console (developer.atlassian.com/console/myapps).

oauth_client_secret.label: OAuth Client Secret
oauth_client_secret.description: The Client Secret from your Atlassian OAuth app. Find this under Settings in the Atlassian Developer Console.

oauth_callback_url.label: OAuth Callback URL
oauth_callback_url.description:
The callback URL configured in your Atlassian OAuth app. This MUST match exactly what you set in the Developer Console under Authorization → OAuth 2.0 (3LO) → Callback URL.

Recommended: use the built-in gateway callback so it works over any network (Tailscale, remote server, etc.):
  http://<your-server-host>:5000/api/gateway/skills/atlassian/oauth/callback

Register that same URL in Atlassian Developer Console. For localhost development you can also use http://localhost:5000/api/gateway/skills/atlassian/oauth/callback.

oauth_scopes.label: OAuth Scopes
oauth_scopes.description:
Space-separated OAuth scopes to request. These must match scopes added under Permissions in the Developer Console.

Common scopes:
  Jira: read:jira-work write:jira-work read:jira-user manage:jira-project manage:jira-configuration
  Confluence: read:confluence-content.all write:confluence-content read:confluence-space.summary
  Bitbucket: repository pullrequest

See: https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/platform/scopes-for-oauth-2-3lo-and-forge-apps/
oauth_scopes.default: read:jira-work write:jira-work read:jira-user manage:jira-project read:confluence-content.all write:confluence-content read:confluence-space.summary offline_access

oauth_access_token.label: OAuth Access Token
oauth_access_token.description: The current OAuth access token. This is automatically populated after completing the OAuth flow, but you can also paste one manually if you obtained it elsewhere.

oauth_refresh_token.label: OAuth Refresh Token
oauth_refresh_token.description: The OAuth refresh token (requires 'offline_access' scope). Used to automatically obtain new access tokens when the current one expires.

_api_token_instructions.label: API Token Setup Instructions
_api_token_instructions.description:
To configure API Token authentication:

1. Go to https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens
2. Click 'Create API token'
3. Give it a label (e.g. 'OrionOmega')
4. Copy the token and paste it below
5. Enter the email address associated with your Atlassian account

api_email.label: Atlassian Account Email
api_email.description: The email address associated with your Atlassian account. Required for Basic auth (email:token).

api_token.label: Personal API Token
api_token.description: Personal API token from id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens. Used with your email for Basic auth.

_site_header.label: ── Atlassian Site ──
_site_header.description: Configure your Atlassian Cloud site connection.

default_cloud_id.label: Atlassian Site URL
default_cloud_id.description: Your Atlassian Cloud site URL (e.g. 'https://yoursite.atlassian.net'). After OAuth authorization, the skill will auto-detect your accessible sites and Cloud ID.

default_jira_project.label: Default Jira Project Key
default_jira_project.description: Jira project key to use when none is specified (e.g. 'PROJ').

default_confluence_space.label: Default Confluence Space
default_confluence_space.description: Confluence space key or ID to use when none is specified.

_products_header.label: ── Product Enablement ──
_products_header.description: Choose which Atlassian products to enable. Disabled products won't load their tools.

enable_jira.label: Enable Jira
enable_jira.description: Enable Jira issue management tools (read, write, search via JQL).

enable_confluence.label: Enable Confluence
enable_confluence.description: Enable Confluence page and space management tools.

enable_compass.label: Enable Compass
enable_compass.description: Enable Compass component management tools. Requires OAuth 2.0 authentication.

enable_jsm.label: Enable Jira Service Management
enable_jsm.description: Enable JSM Ops tools (alerts, schedules, teams).

enable_bitbucket.label: Enable Bitbucket
enable_bitbucket.description: Enable Bitbucket Cloud tools (repos, PRs, pipelines). Requires Bitbucket-specific scopes.

enable_search.label: Enable Cross-Product Search
enable_search.description: Enable Rovo cross-product search and Teamwork Graph tools.

max_results.label: Default Max Results
max_results.description: Default maximum results for search/list operations (1-50).

mcp_endpoint.label: MCP Server Endpoint
mcp_endpoint.description: Atlassian Rovo MCP Server endpoint. Only change if using a custom proxy.
